pi_items.source: Task description and setup script confirm this is the standard save location for the FreeCAD result file

Mathematically calculated: Box 1 (80×60×50 mm at origin) intersected with Box 2 (70×50×50 mm at offset 40,20,0) yields intersection volume of 40×40×50 = 80,000 mm³. Verified against task description which explicitly states 'exactly 80,000 mm³'.
Tolerance of 0.05 (5%) is a standard engineering tolerance for CAD modeling tasks. Consistent with verifier logic at export_result.sh line 31.
Task description explicitly states Box 1 dimensions are '80x60x50 mm'. Metadata matches description exactly.
Task description explicitly states Box 2 dimensions are '70x50x50 mm'. Metadata matches description exactly.

privileged_info_summary: The task is a FreeCAD geometric modeling exercise where the agent must create two overlapping rectangular boxes and compute their Boolean intersection. Box 1 (80×60×50 mm at origin 0,0,0) and Box 2 (70×50×50 mm at position 40,20,0) have an intersection volume of exactly 80,000 mm³. This is verified mathematically: the intersection spans 40 mm in X-direction (from 40 to 80), 40 mm in Y-direction (from 20 to 60), and 50 mm in Z-direction (from 0 to 50), yielding 40×40×50 = 80,000 mm³. The result must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/workspace_intersection.FCStd with a volume tolerance of ±5% (76,000 to 84,000 mm³).
